live_path.role: Parses --owner and forwards it into the service layer.
Orchestrates owner resolution, record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives effective_owner and owner_source from explicit input or defaults.
Builds the stored base record after owner has already been resolved.
Computes routing_key from the resolved owner and name.
Emits owner_source and routing_key by copying the record and appending derived fields.

owner_source.logic: Returns explicit for a non-empty owner argument, otherwise default.

routing_key.logic: Slugifies owner and name, then joins them as owner:name.

emission.logic: Copies the stored record and appends owner_source and routing_key before returning the payload.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner input produces owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and produces owner_source=default with routing_key derived from that default owner.
CLI main forwards --owner through sync_item and the emitted payload preserves the explicit derivations.
Artifact contract requires schema_version cnb55.request_path_map.v1, variant_id from .scenario_variant, and non-empty live_path, test_observations, and rejected_decoys.

rejected_decoys.reason: Storage does not derive owner_source; sync_app.store.make_record only writes name, status, and owner.
routing_key is built in sync_app.service.sync_item after owner resolution, using the owner value forwarded from sync_app.cli.main.
This helper drafts a value from an existing record but is never called by the live sync path.
Legacy helper is not referenced by sync_app.service.sync_item and therefore not part of the live request path.
Legacy routing-key builder is unused in the live path; sync_app.service.sync_item calls sync_app.serializer.build_routing_key instead.
